summary: Implemented M3 appointment, bulk appointment, enfeoffment, office policy, jurisdiction policy, and decision scaffold command/query paths in protocol and sim-core. Commands are shared for player and AI actors, validate authority from polity/office actor ids, preserve policies across holder changes, and reject bulk appointment batches atomically with stable per-item reason strings.

tests_run.result: Modified implementation/test files plus pre-existing modified project coordination files; new tests/sim-core-m3-appointment-enfeoffment.test.ts is untracked.
11 test files passed; 63 tests passed. Includes appointment/enfeoffment property and invariant coverage.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root Vitest 21 files/121 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001

decisions: R3 surface: extended existing state.m3 substrate instead of adding a new root or touching save-format; requestSaveV1 continues refusing M3 state.
Authority is validated from actor.id values of form polity:<id> or office:<id>; UI/player/AI callers use the same GameCommand family.
Decision scaffolds expose coarse reason codes and integer executionPerformanceBps only; no hidden full scoring weights or autonomous planner behavior were added.
Bulk appointment validation sorts items by itemId for stable audit/rejection order and commits only after all items validate.
risks: Appointment audit records include commandId and actor for auditability, so otherwise identical player and AI commands can intentionally produce different state hashes.
M3 save compatibility remains intentionally unsupported by the existing M3 save refusal guard; no save-format migration was attempted.
R3 command/state changes require systems_architect and QA review before acceptance.
requested_action: Review R3 protocol/state/command architecture, authority validation, deterministic bulk semantics, scaffold visibility, and confirm QA routing.
